channel.title: Philips — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Philips (philips).

item.title: CVE-2025-3426 · 7.2 HIGH — Use of default hardcoded credentials
item.description: We observed that Intellispace Portal binaries doesn’t have any protection mechanisms to prevent reverse engineering. Specifically, the app’s code is not obfuscated, and no measures are in place to protect against decompilation, disassembly, or debugg…

item.title: CVE-2025-3425 · 7.3 HIGH — Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution via .NET Deserialization
item.description: The IntelliSpace portal application utilizes .NET Remoting for its functionality. The vulnerability arises from the exploitation of port 755 through the deserialization vulnerability. After analyzing the configuration files, we observed that the serv…

item.title: CVE-2025-3424 · 7.7 HIGH — 3.2.1 Arbitrary File Read in insecure .NET Remoting TCP Channel
item.description: The IntelliSpace portal application utilizes .NET Remoting for its functionality. The vulnerability arises from the exploitation of port 755 through the "Object Marshalling" technique, which allows an attacker to read internal files without any authe…
